name: Engineering
description: From Imhotep's pyramids to Brunel's bridges to the moon — major figures, branches, and breakthroughs.

questions.q: From which Latin word is the term 'engineering' derived?
Which branch of engineering primarily designs roads, bridges, dams, and other public infrastructure?
Which engineering branch deals with engines, machines, and mechanical systems?
Which engineering branch focuses on circuits, motors, generators, and electronics?
Which engineering branch combines chemistry and biology to produce industrial-scale materials and chemicals?
Who is widely considered the earliest civil engineer known by name, designer of the Step Pyramid of Djoser?

source.notes: Worked under Pharaoh Djoser, c. 2630–2611 BCE.

questions.q: Which ancient civilization is famous for building large-scale stone aqueducts to supply cities with water?

source.notes: Roman aqueducts used gravity-fed flow over many kilometers.

questions.q: Who is often called 'the father of civil engineering' for his work on bridges, canals, and the Eddystone Lighthouse?

source.notes: 1724–1792. Coined the term 'civil engineer' to distinguish from 'military'.

questions.q: Which Victorian engineer designed the Great Western Railway, the SS Great Britain, and the Clifton Suspension Bridge?

source.notes: Voted #2 in the BBC's 2002 'Greatest Britons' poll.

questions.q: Who built the first commercial atmospheric steam engine in 1712, predating James Watt's improvements?

source.notes: Used to pump water out of mines.

questions.q: What kind of engine, perfected in the late 19th century, replaced steam in most vehicles by burning fuel inside cylinders?

source.notes: Otto, Daimler, and Diesel were pioneers.

questions.q: Which physical phenomenon — first reliably exceeded by Chuck Yeager in 1947 — describes the resistance felt as an aircraft approaches the speed of sound?

source.notes: Yeager flew the Bell X-1 to Mach 1.06.

questions.q: Which iconic American 1936 dam on the Colorado River is one of the largest engineering projects of the 20th century?

source.notes: Originally Boulder Dam; renamed for President Herbert Hoover.

questions.q: Which simple machine, an early balance device, originated about 5,000 years ago in the Near East?
Which 19th-century invention vastly accelerated electrical engineering as a discipline?
What name is given to the major historical period (~1760–1840) of mechanized industry, canals, and steam power?
Which engineering discipline applies biological systems to medical imaging, prosthetics, and tissue engineering?
Which Greek mathematician famously formulated the principles of buoyancy and the lever?

source.notes: Archimedes of Syracuse, c. 287–212 BCE. 'Give me a lever and a place to stand…'

questions.q: What was the name of George and Robert Stephenson's 1829 locomotive that won the Rainhill Trials?

source.notes: Established locomotives over horse-drawn alternatives for the Liverpool & Manchester Railway.

questions.q: What kind of structure, made famous by builders like Gustave Eiffel, uses iron lattice frames to span large distances?

source.notes: Eiffel Tower (1889) is the most famous wrought-iron lattice example.
